title: Bridge Essay: Lack of action monitoring as a prerequisite for habitual and chunked behavior: Behavioral and neural correlates.
concept: The mechanical mind — neuroscience of automaticity revealing how we become trapped in behavioral loops.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** The mechanical mind — neuroscience of automaticity revealing how we become trapped in behavioral loops.

**Lack of action monitoring as a prerequisite for habitual and chunked behavior: Behavioral and neural correlates.** - iScience (2023). DOI: 10.1016/j.isci.2022.105818. PMID: 36636348. We previously reported the rapid development of habitual behavior in a discrete-trials instrumental task in which lever insertion and retraction act as reward-predictive cues delineating sequence execution. Here we asked whether lever cues or performance variables reflective of skill and automaticity might account for habitual behavior in male rats. Behavior in the discrete-trials habit-promoting task was compared with two task variants lacking the sequence-delineating cues of lever extension an
